# UC-21.2.18 · Golden Batch Comparison and Process Parameter Deviation

## Description

While batches run, the search averages live temperature, pressure, pH, and mix speed by batch, recipe, and phase, joins each segment to approved golden-profile targets from a lookup, computes percent deviation for temperature and pressure, and surfaces batches breaching configurable drift limits.

## Value

Quality and operations teams can intervene during the run—adjust heating, investigate instrument drift, or hold the batch for lab release—rather than discovering off-spec product only at final test, which reduces rework, scrap, and the chance of releasing non-conforming goods to customers.

## Implementation

Maintain `golden_batch_profiles.csv` with one row per `recipe_id` and `phase` including `golden_temp`, `golden_pressure`, and optionally pH tolerances once instrument noise is modeled. Normalize engineering units consistently between historian tags and the profile. **Domain context:** ISA-88 phase naming must match historian phase codes. **Splunk:** `inputlookup` is static—refresh via scheduled `outputlookup` from the validated recipe management system when recipes change.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Time-aligned historian or batch records on `index=process` with `batch_id`, `recipe_id`, `phase`, and `batch_status`.
• A governed lookup `golden_batch_profiles.csv` in `lookups/` with golden setpoints and version metadata; RBAC so only quality engineering can publish updates.
• Network path for Edge Hub, OPC-UA collector, or DB Connect per site security policy.

Create search
• Verify `batch_status=in_progress` values match operator screens; alias legacy values if needed.
• Load the SPL, confirm the join keys (`recipe_id`, `phase`) are upper/lower-case consistent (`| eval recipe_id=upper(recipe_id)` if required).
• Save as alert with throttle per `batch_id` to reduce duplicate pages during noisy phases.

Understanding this SPL
Events from two sourcetypes roll into `stats` averages per batch slice. `inputlookup` brings golden references; `eval` computes absolute percent deviations. The `where` applies temperature and pressure gates; descending sort favors the hottest deviations first.

Validate
• Replay one completed golden batch from last month: deviations should remain under thresholds throughout.
• For a seeded fault test batch, confirm the alert fires within the steady-state slice quality expects.
• Compare averages to operator trend displays for one phase minute-by-minute.

Operationalize
• Near-real-time schedule (1–5 min) routed to shift supervisor; include runbook links for permissive deviations vs mandatory holds.
• Dashboard with batch selector and deviation history; archive fired events to an evidence index tied to electronic batch records.
• Troubleshooting: null golden rows mean missing lookup keys; divide-by-zero in percent eval means zero golden setpoints should be forbidden at publish time.

## SPL

```spl
index=process sourcetype IN ("scada:batch_record","historian:tag") batch_status="in_progress"
| stats avg(temperature) as avg_temp, avg(pressure) as avg_pressure, avg(ph) as avg_ph, avg(mix_speed) as avg_speed by batch_id, recipe_id, phase
| join type=left recipe_id, phase [| inputlookup golden_batch_profiles.csv]
| eval temp_dev=round(abs(avg_temp-golden_temp)/golden_temp*100, 1),
       pressure_dev=round(abs(avg_pressure-golden_pressure)/golden_pressure*100, 1)
| where temp_dev > 5 OR pressure_dev > 8
| sort -temp_dev
```

## Visualization

Line chart with reference bands (live avg_temp vs golden by phase timeline), Heatmap (batch_id × phase deviation %), Table (batch_id, recipe_id, phase, temp_dev, pressure_dev), Single value (batches exceeding threshold in last 24h).

## Known False Positives

Brief oscillations during controlled ramp steps can exceed percent limits until the phase steady-state window is applied—consider `streamstats` or minimum dwell time. Sensor calibration drift makes the process look wrong when the golden profile is still valid. Recipe revisions without lookup refresh flag false drift. Low `golden_pressure` values explode percentage math; guard with minimum denominators. Parallel equipment trains sharing a recipe ID but different physical gains need train-specific keys.
